mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 3 Series 320e engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 320e engine (B48B20A with hybrid system) typically costs £2,500‑£3,800 supply only. Fitting adds £1,000‑£1,500. As a PHEV, the hybrid system components add complexity and cost. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your vehicle.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW 3 Series 320e engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B48 is a robust and refined engine – the timing chain issues that plagued the N20 are not a documented concern on the B48. However, the plug‑in hybrid system adds complexity. HV battery degradation and charging system failures are the primary concerns. With regular maintenance and proper battery care, the 320e can be a reliable and efficient powertrain.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 3 Series 320e?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most common issues are PHEV‑specific: HV battery degradation (reduced electric range), KLE module/charging system failure, and premature brake wear due to the vehicle's weight. The B48 engine itself is generally reliable, though oil leaks (valve cover, oil filter housing) can occur.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a 320e engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Given the 3 Series's retained value (typically £15,000‑£35,000+), a £2,500‑£4,800 reconditioned engine is a significant but often justifiable expense. However, if the HV battery has also failed (costing £2,000‑£5,000+), the total repair cost may be substantial. Get a full diagnostic assessment before committing – if the rest of the vehicle is in good condition, replacement is usually the better option.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my 3 Series's engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The 320e uses the B48B20A 2.0‑litre turbo petrol engine combined with an 80 kW electric motor. This is stamped on the engine block and listed on your V5C. The 320e badge itself confirms the powertrain – it's the 204 PS plug‑in hybrid offered in the G20 3 Series from 2021 onwards.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair a 320e engine issue, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most issues – oil leaks, charging system failures, brake wear – are repairable. HV battery degradation may be covered under warranty if the vehicle is still within the warranty period (typically 8 years/100,000 miles). However, if the engine suffers major internal failure, a full engine replacement is usually more cost‑effective than a rebuild.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the G20 320e generation. The hybrid system components (electric motor, battery, inverter) add complexity and cost to any replacement. PHEV-specific labour is typically higher than for conventional engines.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 320e engine typically takes 12‑16 hours at £75‑£110/hr (independent BMW specialist). Hybrid system integration adds to the complexity.

problems.title: HV (High‑Voltage) Battery Degradation
problems.symptoms: Reduced electric‑only range (noted drop from 33‑38 miles to 20‑25 miles over time), longer charging times, hybrid system warnings on the dashboard, the car running only on petrol.
problems.typicalMileage: 30,000‑60,000 miles (age‑related as much as mileage — the 12 kWh battery is subject to degradation over time).
problems.replacementCost: N/A — battery replacement only (if ignored, the vehicle runs on petrol only, reducing fuel economy).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if HV battery warnings appear or if electric range drops significantly.

problems.recommendation: The 320e's 12 kWh lithium‑ion battery is a significant component. Over time, battery degradation is inevitable — the range will drop, and charging may take longer. BMW batteries are generally well‑engineered, but if the battery fails outside of the warranty period (typically 6‑8 years), replacement is expensive. If you notice a significant drop in electric range or warning lights, have the battery health checked by a specialist. Some owners report that the 320e's hybrid system is less sophisticated than competitors' offerings, and battery degradation can be a concern on higher‑mileage examples.

problems.title: Charging System / KLE Module Failure
problems.symptoms: The car fails to charge or charges very slowly, charging port faults, dashboard warnings, the car running only on petrol.
problems.typicalMileage: Any mileage — can occur at any time.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if the car fails to charge.

problems.recommendation: The KLE (Comfort Charging Electronics Controller) module is a common failure point on BMW PHEVs. If the car fails to charge or charges slowly, the KLE module may be faulty. Charging port/socket faults are also reported. This is a specialist repair — a diagnostic check will confirm the issue. If your 320e won't charge, don't ignore it — the car will run on petrol only, negating the benefits of the hybrid system.

problems.title: Brake Wear (Regenerative Braking & Weight)
problems.symptoms: Squealing brakes, reduced braking performance, warning lights, high brake pad wear.
problems.typicalMileage: 20,000‑40,000 miles (the 320e's 1,845 kg kerb weight places extra stress on brakes).

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if brake wear is noted.

problems.recommendation: The 320e is a heavy car — the battery adds significant weight. Premature wear of brakes and suspension is a common issue on PHEVs. Regular brake inspections are essential, and you should budget for more frequent brake replacements than on a conventional 3 Series. The regenerative braking system can also cause uneven pad wear.

problems.title: B48 Engine Oil Leaks (Valve Cover / Oil Filter Housing)
problems.symptoms: Oil spots under the vehicle, burning oil smell, low oil level warning, visible oil leak from the top or front of the engine.
problems.repairCost: £300‑£800 [PLACEHOLDER] (valve cover gasket or oil filter housing gasket replacement).

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if oil leak is detected.

problems.recommendation: The B48 is a robust engine, but oil leaks from the valve cover gasket and oil filter housing are not uncommon. If the oil filter housing gasket fails, oil can leak onto the serpentine belt, causing it to slip or break — which can then be ingested into the engine through the front crankshaft seal, causing catastrophic failure. If you notice oil spots under your 320e or a burning oil smell, investigate immediately.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW 3 Series 320e — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: May be covered under warranty; if not, assess cost vs. vehicle value
Worth fixing — the car needs to charge to be a PHEV
Full engine replacement often more cost‑effective

buyingChecks.title: Buying This BMW 3 Series 320e? Check These First
buyIf: * Full BMW service history with correct hybrid system maintenance
* HV battery health check completed and documented
* Evidence of regular charging and battery care
* Warranty remaining — the 320e is a complex, expensive vehicle to repair out of warranty
avoidIf: * Any sign of HV battery warnings or reduced electric range
* No evidence of regular charging — the hybrid battery may have been neglected
* Poor service history — these vehicles demand strict maintenance
* Any sign of oil leaks — the B48 is robust, but oil leaks are a known issue

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The BMW 320e is a compelling entry‑level plug‑in hybrid — combining the proven B48 2.0‑litre turbo petrol engine with an 80 kW electric motor for 204 PS and 350 Nm. It offers genuine electric‑only capability for short commutes (up to 38 miles) and excellent fuel economy when charged regularly. However, PHEV complexity brings potential issues — battery degradation, charging system failures, and the extra weight placing stress on brakes and suspension. Buy on service history above all else — a vehicle with documented battery health checks and regular maintenance is a safe bet; a neglected example can be expensive to repair.

cta.label: → Compare 3 Series 320e engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 3 Series 320e (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Electric Range — Does the displayed range match expectations? (32‑38 miles claimed, real‑world ~20‑30 miles)
2. Service History — Full BMW history with hybrid system maintenance records
3. HV Battery Health — Has the battery been tested? What's its state of health?
4. Charging — Does the car charge correctly? (KLE module and charging port check)
5. Oil Leaks — Check under the vehicle and around the valve cover and oil filter housing
6. Brake Condition — Any squealing or wear? (the 320e is heavy on brakes)
7. Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any hybrid system or engine codes?
8. MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures or any advisory notes
